headline: Statutory Bodies Accumulated RM14B in Unexplained Asset Gains
context: The Auditor General's 2025 report flagged RM14 billion in unexplained asset valuation gains across 18 statutory bodies, with land revaluations, investment mark-ups, and interagency transfers lacking proper documentation or independent verification.

cards.big: RM14 billion in unexplained asset gains across 18 statutory bodies — public money growing on paper with no audit trail
cards.sub: Land revaluations alone account for RM8.2 billion — assets marked up without independent valuer confirmation

cards.big: Statutory bodies control RM480 billion in combined assets — larger than many sovereign wealth funds — with patchy audit coverage
cards.sub: Only 42% of statutory bodies received unqualified audit opinions in 2025 — 58% had material issues flagged

cards.big: Asset inflation allows statutory bodies to report healthy balance sheets while operational efficiency deteriorates
cards.sub: Public perception of well-managed agencies masks underlying governance failures hidden by inflated valuations

cards.big: AG has no power to compel corrective action — audit findings are advisory, requiring only 'notation' by parliament
cards.sub: PAC reviewed only 8 of 42 flagged agencies in 2025 — 34 audit findings effectively went without parliamentary scrutiny

cards.big: When public agencies mark up their own assets without verification, it's not accounting — it's fiction with an official stamp
Should statutory body asset revaluations require mandatory independent valuation and PAC review above RM100 million threshold?
